methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Claros operates in Climate & Energy at the Seed stage, having raised $30M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of nine private frontier-tech companies in the sector plus one public reference (LNZA), including peers such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression uses the $30M total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier to project forward valuation, implying $75M. Peer-Median analysis benchmarks Claros against three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A). The median valuation across these three peers is $150M, with a range of $110M to $200M.

Triangulating these two methods yields a valuation range of $64M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $112M. The discrepancy between methods reflects uncertainty in both the appropriate stage-to-stage progression rate for seed-stage climate tech and the applicability of Series A comparables to a company still in Seed. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to peers, the timing and scale of the next funding round, and sector-specific market conditions. The $112M median represents the most defensible point estimate given the available data.
